title: anthyllis atropurpurea
intro: Anthyllis atropurpurea, also known as kidney vetch, is a perennial legume native to Europe and the Mediterranean region. The plant's leaves and flowers have been used in herbal medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Anthyllis atropurpurea are said to include relief from kidney stones, urinary tract infections, and other urinary issues, as well as potential anti-aging and immune system benefits. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and flowers are often dried and used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
